name: Nabil Bank Limited
description: Nabil Bank Limited is one of Nepal's largest private-sector commercial banks, founded in 1984 as the country's first joint-venture bank. Headquartered in Kathmandu, Nabil offers a full range of retail, corporate, and SME banking services across a nationwide branch network, and is a key constituent of the NEPSE benchmark index.
highlights: Nepal's first joint-venture commercial bank, established 1984
Listed on NEPSE under the Commercial Banks sub-index
Among the largest banks in Nepal by deposits and market capitalization
Pays regular cash and bonus share dividends to shareholders

name: Nepal Reinsurance Company Limited
description: Nepal Reinsurance Company Limited (Nepal Re) is the national reinsurance company of Nepal, providing reinsurance capacity to domestic life and non-life insurers as well as cross-border cedants. Established under the Insurance Act and majority-owned by the Government of Nepal, Nepal Re is a strategically important constituent of the NEPSE insurance sub-indices.
highlights: Nepal's first and only domestic reinsurance company
Majority government ownership, with Nepal Rastra Bank and public stake
Underwrites treaty and facultative reinsurance for the domestic market
Listed on NEPSE under the Non-Life Insurance sub-index

name: Nepal Life Insurance Company Limited
description: Nepal Life Insurance Company Limited is one of the largest private-sector life insurers in Nepal, offering endowment, term, and investment-linked life insurance products through a nationwide agency network. Listed on NEPSE since 2005, Nepal Life is widely held by retail investors and is a benchmark constituent of the Life Insurance sub-index.
highlights: Among the largest private life insurers in Nepal by gross premium
Listed on NEPSE under the Life Insurance sub-index since 2005
Distributes products through a nationwide network of agents and branches
Consistent dividend payer with strong retail shareholder base
